title: The Oak and the Reed
lines: An Oak grew in a meadow beside a River where a Reed also grew.
The Oak was strong and sturdy, with deep roots and thick trunk.
The Reed was slender and bowed easily with every wind.
"How can you stand so weak and bent?" asked the Oak.
"I admire my own strength; look at my thick trunk and strong branches!"
"I pity you," replied the Reed, "your stubbornness will be your ruin."
Soon a terrible storm came up.
The Oak stood rigid and fought against the wind.
The Reed bent and bowed with the storm.
The violent winds snapped the great Oak in two, and it fell to the ground.
The Reed, bending and swaying, came through the storm unharmed.
moral: Flexibility is better than stubborn rigidity.
